nameMeanings.name: Muschet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  Norman, Scottish: locative name from Montfiquet (Calvados).
2  English: there has been some confusion with   Muskett  .

variants.name: Muskett
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French mouschet, mousquet ‘male (tercel) of a small hawk, especially a sparrowhawk’, from a diminutive of mouche ‘fly’.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: Henry Muschet was born in 1210, in Fen Ditton, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of William Muschet II. He had at least 1 son. He died on 9 July 1251, in his hometown, at the age of 41.

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Muschet(은)는 1210년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 펜 딧튼에서 William Muschet II의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1251년 7월 9일 그의 고향에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.
